original_abstract:
Deep learning developments in remote sensing change detection (CD) have been primarily driven by Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers. While CNNs are limited by a restricted receptive field that struggles to capture long-range spatial dependencies, Transformers suffer from quadratic computational complexity, making them resource-intensive for large-scale high-resolution datasets. In this paper, we explore the potential of the Mamba architecture—a state space model (SSM) featuring linear scaling and a selective scan mechanism—to overcome these limitations in CD tasks. 

We introduce ChangeMamba, a family of spatio-temporal state space frameworks tailored for three core CD subtasks: MambaBCD for binary change detection, MambaSCD for semantic change detection, and MambaBDA for building damage assessment. Each framework utilizes the cutting-edge Visual Mamba (VMamba) as a weight-sharing encoder to extract global spatial context from multi-temporal images. To effectively capture temporal dynamics, we propose three spatio-temporal relationship modeling mechanisms—sequential, cross, and parallel modeling—which enable the Mamba-based decoder to learn complex interactions between temporal features efficiently. 

Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets (SYSU-CD, LEVIR-CD+, WHU-CD, SECOND, and xBD) demonstrate that ChangeMamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art CNN- and Transformer-based methods in accuracy and efficiency. Furthermore, our architecture exhibits superior robustness against degraded input data, such as noise and blur. The results suggest that the Mamba architecture is a powerful and efficient alternative for remote sensing dense prediction tasks. Our source code is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.
humanized_abstract:
See how Deep learning advances in Remote Sensing (RS) Change Detection (CD) have mainly relied on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers. While CNNs are restricted by a fixed-sized receptive field which is unable to fully exploit long-range spatial dependencies, Transformers are limited by their quadratic computational cost which does not scale well with large high-resolution images. In this work, we investigate the capabilities of Mamba, a state space model (SSM) that enjoys linear scaling and a selective scan mechanism, on CD problems.

ChangeMamba: A Unified Family of Spatio-temporal State Space Models for Core CD Tasks Our goal is to develop a unified family of spatio-temporal state space models that are applicable to three fundamental CD subtasks, namely ChangeMambaBCD (MambaBCD for brevity) for the Change Detection (CD) subtask of binary change detection, ChangeMambaSCD (MambaSCD for brevity) for the CD subtask of semantic change detection, and ChangeMambaBDA (MambaBDA for brevity) for the CD subtask of building damage assessment. Our solutions leverage the merits of the state-of-the-art Visual Mamba (VMamba) as a weight-sharing encoder to ingest the global spatial information in the multi-temporal images. Furthermore, we develop three spatio-temporal relationship modeling mechanisms namely, sequential, cross, and parallel modeling, to enable the Mamba-based decoder to leverage the merits of each modeling mechanism for capturing the intricate temporal relationships among the temporal features.

Experiments are conducted on five widely used change detection datasets including SYSU-CD, LEVIR-CD+, WHU-CD, SECOND and xBD. The results verify that ChangeMamba outperforms state-of-the-art approaches of other deep learning architectures including CNNs and Transformers in terms of detection accuracy and efficiency, while remaining higher robustness against noisy and blurred input images. Overall, our proposed Mamba architecture has shown state-of-the-art performance for remote sensing dense prediction tasks. The corresponding code is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.

document.input:
Deep learning developments in remote sensing change detection (CD) have been primarily driven by Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers. While CNNs are limited by a restricted receptive field that struggles to capture long-range spatial dependencies, Transformers suffer from quadratic computational complexity, making them resource-intensive for large-scale high-resolution datasets. In this paper, we explore the potential of the Mamba architecture—a state space model (SSM) featuring linear scaling and a selective scan mechanism—to overcome these limitations in CD tasks. 

We introduce ChangeMamba, a family of spatio-temporal state space frameworks tailored for three core CD subtasks: MambaBCD for binary change detection, MambaSCD for semantic change detection, and MambaBDA for building damage assessment. Each framework utilizes the cutting-edge Visual Mamba (VMamba) as a weight-sharing encoder to extract global spatial context from multi-temporal images. To effectively capture temporal dynamics, we propose three spatio-temporal relationship modeling mechanisms—sequential, cross, and parallel modeling—which enable the Mamba-based decoder to learn complex interactions between temporal features efficiently. 

Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets (SYSU-CD, LEVIR-CD+, WHU-CD, SECOND, and xBD) demonstrate that ChangeMamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art CNN- and Transformer-based methods in accuracy and efficiency. Furthermore, our architecture exhibits superior robustness against degraded input data, such as noise and blur. The results suggest that the Mamba architecture is a powerful and efficient alternative for remote sensing dense prediction tasks. Our source code is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.
